$description: NCE Design System theme for business web applications and Frappe applications. Hue-based color system with AI agent guidance.

agentGuidance.purpose: This theme defines a complete color system for business web applications. Use these tokens consistently across all UI elements.

colorUsage.primary: Brand color for CTAs, links, interactive elements. Use shade 500 as default, lighter shades for hover states, darker for active states.
colorUsage.neutral: Backgrounds, borders, structural elements. Use lighter shades (50-200) for backgrounds, darker (700-900) for text.
colorUsage.semantic: Status colors ONLY for their designated purpose. Never use success for decoration, error for emphasis, or warning for information.

accessibilityRules.textContrast: Minimum 4.5:1 for body text, 3:1 for large text (18px+ or 14px bold)
accessibilityRules.colorAlone: Never use color as the only indicator of state or meaning

implementationNotes.cssVariables: All tokens available as CSS custom properties (--primary-500, --neutral-100, etc.)
implementationNotes.hslValues: Raw HSL values provided in $extensions.nce-hsl for programmatic manipulation

primary.$description: Primary brand color. Use for CTAs, links, interactive elements, and brand emphasis.

hue.$description: Primary hue value (0-360 degrees on color wheel)

defaultShade.$description: Default shade for solid primary buttons (50-900)

outlineShade.$description: Shade for outline button border (shift-click to set)

textFlipShade.$description: Shade threshold where button text flips from dark to light

shades.$description: Full primary shade scale - all available for designer use

neutral.$description: Neutral colors for backgrounds, borders, and structural elements.

hue.$description: Neutral hue value (-1 for pure gray, 0-360 for tinted)

shades.$description: Full neutral shade scale - all available for designer use

success.$description: Positive outcomes, completed actions, valid states

windowControls.$description: Window control button colors (macOS traffic light style)

frappeCSS.$description: Frappe CSS custom properties - use these variable names exactly
frappeCSS.$usage: Python script reads these directly into app CSS
